creator: rfarmer@predatorlabs.net
text:
On Mon, Feb 15, 2010 at 2:22 AM, wen heping <wenheping@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>>
>>
>> I think that there ought to be an option to build without the gui, for
>
> I agree an option without gui.
>
>
>>
>> those people who only need the CLI, and want to avoid the java
>> dependencies. =A0I did this when I first made a port for 5.0, and the
>> java dependencies weren't in the tree.
>>
>> What happens if fftw isn't used? =A0Is their some built-in fft code,
>> and, if so, how does it compare in speed and generality to fftw? =A0If
>
> I think fftw should be a option and its default should be ON.

Ok - I updated the patch and turned fftw on by default.

--=20
Rob Farmer

>
>>
>> there isn't, or the built-in code is markedly slower or less flexible
>> than fftw, then this ought to be enabled by default, as the fft is
>> important in a lot of applications, and is not very costly in terms of
>> added disk space and build time.
>>
>> Is the tcl-tk option still relevant, now that the gui is mostly
>> java-based? Are there still some wrappers to install?
>>
>> Will any dependent ports (toolboxes) need any of the components that
>> are now disabled by default? If so, then we need to enable them by
>
> Here need much more work. Beside toolboxes, I think many other scilab
> modules should be ported into portstree.
>
>
>>
>> default, or go through the hassle of defining PKGSUFFIXes and slave
>> ports.
>>
>> b.
>
>
